sequence.title: WAVE 1 — Emergency & Foundation (Days 1-3)
sequence.rationale: Establish urgency and preserve rights. Emergency motions get immediate attention.

filings.title: Emergency Motion — Parenting Time
filings.why_first: Emergency motion gets immediate judicial review. Establishes that father is actively seeking contact with child. Creates urgency that supports all other filings.

filings.title: Judicial Disqualification Motion
filings.why_first: Must be filed BEFORE any substantive hearing. If McNeill is disqualified, all subsequent hearings are before a new judge. MCR 2.003(D) — file within 14 days of discovering bias.
filings.dependencies: None — but must precede any hearing on F1/F7
filings.concurrent: File same day as F1 to ensure new judge hears emergency motion
filings.expected_response: 21 days — chief judge reviews if McNeill refuses

sequence.title: WAVE 2 — Administrative Complaints (Days 3-5)
sequence.rationale: No filing fees, no court deadlines. These are investigations, not litigation. Zero risk.

filings.title: JTC Complaint — Judicial Misconduct
filings.why_now: Free to file. Creates external pressure on McNeill. JTC investigation is independent of court proceedings. Supports F3 disqualification narrative.
filings.dependencies: F3 filed first (shows good faith — tried court process before JTC)
filings.concurrent: Can mail same day as F10

filings.title: AGC Grievance — Attorney Misconduct
filings.why_now: Free to file. Documents Barnes misconduct for the record. If Barnes participated in fraud, AGC investigation strengthens §1983 conspiracy claim.

sequence.title: WAVE 3 — Core Litigation (Days 7-10)
sequence.rationale: After emergency + disqualification are filed, proceed with substantive claims.

filings.title: Fraud Upon the Court Complaint
filings.why_now: Foundation for everything else. Establishes that original PPO/custody proceedings were fraudulent. Supports void order argument in F7.

filings.title: Custody Modification Motion + Brief
filings.why_now: After fraud complaint establishes narrative, seek custody modification. Together with F1 (emergency) creates two-pronged custody strategy.

sequence.title: WAVE 4 — Bypass Courts (Days 14-21)
sequence.rationale: If 14th Circuit is unresponsive or hostile, escalate to higher courts and federal.

filings.title: 42 USC §1983 Federal Civil Rights
filings.method: CM/ECF (PACER) or in person at Grand Rapids
filings.why_now: Federal court is the nuclear option. File after giving state court a chance (14 days). This timing also helps with Younger abstention defense — you tried state remedies first.
filings.expected_response: 21 days for defendants to respond (if IFP granted)

filings.title: MSC Complaint for Superintending Control
filings.why_now: Last resort for state courts. File after federal to show MSC that parallel federal action exists. Creates maximum pressure on 14th Circuit.

sequence.title: WAVE 5 — Appellate (Days 14-30)
sequence.rationale: COA filings proceed on their own timeline. File when briefs are polished.

filings.title: COA Appeal Brief (366810)
filings.why_now: COA 366810 already filed. Brief deadline may be approaching. Check with COA clerk for current deadline.

filings.title: COA Application for Leave (New)
filings.why_now: Separate from 366810. Covers additional issues not in original appeal. File after F9 so COA sees consistent narrative.
filings.expected_response: 28 days for response; decision in 2-6 months
